description:
bl-2148 noted the same claim-time race exists on review and close, and flagged the parallel as a follow-up. With the participant primitive (bl-eae4) and trait (bl-1ea6) in place, generalize the bl-2148 lifecycle-sync semantics to those two transitions.

## Scope

- review and close go through the participant dispatcher and respect each subscribed participant's failure policy.
- A required participant whose negotiation fails aborts the transition with a clear message (matching bl-2148's hard-fail-on-unreachable stance).
- Best-effort participants warn and continue, preserving today's behavior for legacy plugins under the shim.

## UX

When a transition is aborted by a required participant's failure, the error message states which participant failed and why, and how to override (--no-sync per-invocation, or local config).

## Tests

- Happy path: review and close succeed when all subscribed participants succeed.
- Required-fail: a required participant rejecting causes the transition to abort; state remains pre-transition.
- Best-effort fail: a best-effort participant rejecting yields a warning; transition completes.
- Concurrency: review against a remote that's been updated mid-flight retries via the negotiation primitive.
title: Apply participant negotiation to review and close transitions
